title: Gekto system

The '''Gekto system''' was a [[Star system/Legends|star system]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] containing the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Hoszh Iszhir]], [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Gektl]] species.<ref name="EAOC" /><ref name="Blog" /> [[Talon Karrde]] was making shipping arrangements in the Gekto system when [[Ja Bardrin]] captured the crew of Karrde's [[Starship/Legends|ship]], the [[Wild Karrde]], and forced his second-in-command [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]] to rescue [[Sansia Bardrin]] from the [[Drach'nam]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Chay Praysh]].<ref name="JS">{{StoryCite|story=Jade Solitaire|book=Tales from the New Republic}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|The Gektls being from the Gekto system strikes me as both a cool idea and a logical one.|Jason Fry|<ref name="Atlas Discussion 3">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27848315|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2007-12-11|time=7:59 AM|archivedate=20220621142853|quote=The Gektls being from the Gekto system strikes me as both a cool idea and a logical one.|image=File:JasonFry-JCFAtlasHomeworlds.png}}</ref>}}
The Gekto system was first mentioned in [[Timothy Zahn]]'s short story "[[Jade Solitaire]]."<ref name="JS" />

While preparing ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', authors [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] consulted with fans on the [[Jedi Council Forums]] about planet and system locations. One fan, "Senator_Cilghal," noted the similarity between the name of the Gekto system and the Gektl species, the latter having recently been established as being from the planet Hoszh Iszhir in the [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|''Star Wars'' Hyperspace]] article [[Aliens in the Empire]], written by [[Abel G. Peña]] and [[Rich Handley]], and suggested Hoszh Iszhir be located in the Gekto system.<ref name="Atlas Discussion 1">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27826984|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=Senator_Cilghal|date=2007-12-06|time=2:13 AM|archivedate=20221130102105|quote=If I may suggest a retcon, since Hoszh Iszhir is the GEKTL homeworld, perhaps it is in the GEKTO system mentioned in Jade Solitaire?}}</ref><ref name="AitE">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/97/indexp4.html|series=Aliens in the Empire|text=Part 2: To a Traitor Go the Spoils}}</ref> Abel G. Peña felt the idea made sense, and stated he had no objection to the idea, and doubted co-author Rich Handley would have objections either,<ref name="Atlas Discussion 2">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27829324|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Abel G. Peña|Halagad_Ventor]]|date=2007-12-06|time=2:28 PM|archivedate=20221130102105|quote=That makes sense. I'm not adverse to the Gektl/Gekto idea, and I doubt Rich would be either.}}</ref> and Jason Fry favorably regarded the notion and saw the logic in making the connection between the Gektls and the Gekto system.<ref name="Atlas Discussion 3" /> Upon the publication of ''The Essential Atlas'', Daniel Wallace published a blog, "Unstated Canon in Star Wars: The Essential Atlas," that listed bits of continuity that weren't formally stated in the text of the book but had been vetted by Lucasfilm and were to be regarded as official; among those bits was confirmation that Hoszh Iszhir was in the Gekto system.<ref name="Blog" />
